name: Tate & Lyle PLC
description: A global provider of specialty food ingredients including sweeteners, texturants, and dietary fibres. Sold Primient (Primary Products) in 2022. Acquired CP Kelco (pectin/speciality gums) in Nov 2024 for ~US$1.8bn.

assumptions.revenueDrivers: FY2025 revenue £1,736m (includes CP Kelco £224m from Nov 2024). Pro forma full-year revenue ~£2,124m. Volume-led growth in specialty sweeteners, texturants and CP Kelco pectin/gums. Health and wellness megatrend (sugar reduction, fibre fortification).
assumptions.marginDrivers: Adjusted EBITDA margin expanding: 17.2%→21.9% over 5 years (+350bps). CP Kelco adds high-margin business. FY2025 adjusted operating profit £288m. Productivity savings programme delivering ~US$50m pa.
assumptions.risks: Net debt £961m (2.2x pro forma EBITDA) from CP Kelco. Covenant at 3.5x (temporarily 4.0x). FX exposure (significant USD earnings). Raw material cost volatility. Integration execution risk. 75m new shares diluted existing holders.
assumptions.catalyst: CP Kelco synergy realisation, full-year profit contribution from FY2026. Adjusted EBITDA margin targeting continued expansion. Cash conversion 82% in FY2025 vs 75% target.
